reportingProfile.description: Reporting profile for the provider-backed latency-injection campaign.

baseline.purpose: Provider-backed baseline for LocalAI worker-mode characterization on a proxmox-k3s-generated K3s cluster.
selectionCriteria: Keep the provider-backed baseline intentionally close to the historical application-level baseline so that infrastructure effects are not mixed with model, workload, or placement changes.
Use two RPC workers to preserve the validated LocalAI worker-mode topology before introducing resource, node-count, or placement variation cycles.
Use the smallest validated model family member to minimize footprint during the first provider-backed end-to-end cycle.
Use the controlled low-load workload to validate the full provider-backed pipeline before executing broader sweeps.
Use the explicit PL_COLOCATED placement profile for the first provider-backed baseline; later cycles can switch placement profiles while keeping model, workload, and infrastructure fixed.
lockRules: Do not mutate B1 after it has been used for a frozen provider-backed cycle.
Do not reuse B0 artifacts as evidence for B1.
Do not interpret B1 and B0 results as directly interchangeable without accounting for their different infrastructure profiles.
Use B1 as the provider-backed reference only after the declared proxmox-k3s cluster has passed validation.
Collect minimal observability evidence for B1 using metrics-server, kubectl snapshots and Locust artifacts before diagnosis and reporting.
baseline.notes: B1 intentionally inherits the application-level shape of B0 while switching to the INFRA_C1_1CP_2W_8C16G provider-backed infrastructure profile. This keeps the first provider-backed cycle focused on validating the provisioning-to-benchmark path rather than changing multiple dimensions at once.
baseline.scenarioProfileName: Provider-backed baseline for LocalAI worker-mode characterization on a proxmox-k3s-generated K3s cluster.

payload.description: Provider-backed campaign that keeps infrastructure, model, workload, LocalAI worker count and placement fixed while varying only the controlled network-latency profile.

scope.primaryQuestion: How does progressively injected worker-node network latency affect LocalAI worker-mode latency, throughput and benchmark stability under fixed infrastructure, placement and workload conditions?

stateModel.profileStatus: Configuration-profile lifecycle state. This is the canonical meaning of the backward-compatible status field.
stateModel.configurationState: Whether the cycle configuration is active, locked, superseded, or deprecated.
stateModel.executionState: Latest known execution outcome for the cycle or campaign.
stateModel.status: Backward-compatible alias of profileStatus; execution outcomes must be read from executionState.

infrastructureSummarySemantics.description: The cycle uses one fixed infrastructure profile as the common context for all scenarios.

payload.purpose: Provider-backed fixed infrastructure profile with 4 worker nodes and fixed per-node capacity for evaluating LocalAI worker-mode behavior under controlled network-latency profiles.

lifecycle.policySelection: Cycles may override the profile default only when the override is declared in the cycle profile and captured in the lifecycle manifest.

nodeNaming.notes: Node names are compatibility handles for generated provider-backed node-count variants; infrastructureProfileId remains the authoritative environment identifier.

provisioning.notes: Provider configuration is resolved through the proxmox-k3s provider binding associated with this infrastructure profile.

benchmarkCompatibility.notes: Use this profile for latency-injection campaigns that keep infrastructure, model, workload, LocalAI worker count and placement fixed while varying only the network latency profile applied before benchmark execution.

comparisonPolicy.policy: Use this profile as the cycle-scoped C5 infrastructure identifier; its resource envelope is intentionally equivalent to the fixed four-worker provider-backed infrastructure used in placement studies so C5 can vary only the latency profile while keeping infrastructure and placement fixed.

observabilityAssumptions.notes: Minimal K3s observability is sufficient for this profile unless a later configuration explicitly requires additional add-ons.

placementCompatibility.notes: The C5 default placement profile spreads a fixed four-worker LocalAI topology across four provider worker nodes; C5 variants keep infrastructure, model, workload and placement fixed while varying only the latency profile applied before benchmark execution.

methodologicalEquivalence.equivalenceScope: same logical cluster size, worker-node capacity, naming convention, IP plan and Proxmox placement assumptions; dedicated C5 profile and YAML paths exist for traceability while C5 varies only the network latency profile

methodologicalNotes: C5 intentionally preserves the fixed 4-worker provider-backed infrastructure shape used for C4, while varying only the latency profile applied before benchmark execution.

consolidatedWorkersOnHost.reason: Additional Proxmox hosts are not part of the available execution environment; worker VM placement is therefore materialized on the declared available host.

physicalHostMaterialization.interpretationRule: Infrastructure comparisons must use the declared profile and provider binding; identical Kubernetes node counts do not imply distinct physical Proxmox hosts for every worker VM.

payload.description: Runtime-generated provider-backed variant cycle for LI_L0_NONE within the C5 campaign.

scenario.purpose: Provider-backed latency-injection reference scenario with active reset of any existing netem configuration and no artificial delay.
selectionCriteria: Keep infrastructure, model, workload, LocalAI worker count and placement fixed so the campaign isolates injected network latency.
Use the spread-workers placement to expose server-to-worker communication over multiple provider worker nodes.
Retain failed or unsupported latency-injection variants as instrumentation or network-sensitivity evidence when the requested latency profile cannot be applied or benchmarked.
lockRules: Do not change infrastructure, model, workload, LocalAI worker count or placement inside this scenario.
Do not interpret latency effects without checking benchmark failures, timeouts, cluster-side artifacts and latency-injection manifests.
Use the scenario only within a declared latency-injection campaign or a documented derivative cycle.

templateMetadata.generationPolicy: Only explicit placeholders delimited by double underscores may be substituted. Runtime generation must not perform global literal replacements such as C1 or B1.

latencyProfile.purpose: Reference profile that actively clears any existing netem qdisc and applies no artificial network delay.

target.rationale: Apply latency to provider-backed worker nodes where LocalAI server and RPC workers are scheduled, while keeping the control plane outside the injected path.

methodologicalRole.interpretation: The no-latency profile is an active reference: it resets existing qdisc state and leaves worker-node network paths unmodified before benchmark execution.

scenario.purpose: Provider-backed latency-injection scenario under fixed infrastructure, model, workload, LocalAI worker count and placement.
selectionCriteria: Keep infrastructure, model, workload, LocalAI worker count and placement fixed so the campaign isolates injected network latency.
Use the spread-workers placement to expose server-to-worker communication over multiple provider worker nodes.
Retain failed or unsupported latency-injection variants as instrumentation or network-sensitivity evidence when the requested latency profile cannot be applied or benchmarked.
lockRules: Do not change infrastructure, model, workload, LocalAI worker count or placement inside this scenario.
Do not interpret latency effects without checking benchmark failures, timeouts, cluster-side artifacts and latency-injection manifests.
Use the scenario only within a declared latency-injection campaign or a documented derivative cycle.

latencyProfile.purpose: Moderate latency intended to emulate a near-edge or metro-area distributed environment.

target.rationale: Apply latency to provider-backed worker nodes where LocalAI server and RPC workers are scheduled, while keeping the control plane outside the injected path.

methodologicalRole.interpretation: Latency profiles are used to evaluate how distributed LocalAI worker-mode performance changes when inter-node communication becomes progressively more expensive.

latencyProfile.purpose: Higher latency intended to emulate a remote edge/cloud-edge path while remaining below extreme stress levels.

target.rationale: Apply latency to provider-backed worker nodes where LocalAI server and RPC workers are scheduled, while keeping the control plane outside the injected path.

methodologicalRole.interpretation: Latency profiles are used to evaluate how distributed LocalAI worker-mode performance changes when inter-node communication becomes progressively more expensive.

latencyProfile.purpose: High-latency stress profile intended to expose the point at which distributed worker communication becomes operationally unfavorable.

target.rationale: Apply latency to provider-backed worker nodes where LocalAI server and RPC workers are scheduled, while keeping the control plane outside the injected path.

methodologicalRole.interpretation: Latency profiles are used to evaluate how distributed LocalAI worker-mode performance changes when inter-node communication becomes progressively more expensive.
